title: XoomAI — Founder Strategy Memo | MEMETIK
description: Founder-facing strategy memo for XoomAI focused on XoomAgent™, vertical account selection, outbound-page alignment, and commercial demand capture.
tldr: 0% AI visibility across ChatGPT, Gemini, and Google AI Overviews — not mentioned in any sampled prompts.
14.8M total validated search demand in the category with 310K base expected traffic at 12 months.
Zero organic traffic, zero ranked keywords, zero referring domains — greenfield opportunity.
Buyer guide cluster (140K demand) is the fastest bottom-of-funnel entry point — ai tools, best ai, ai workflow automation.
No competitors own the AI consultancy + managed AI employee niche in AU — wide open.
6-month execution: decision pages month 1, comparison/review coverage month 2-3, compound distribution months 4-6.

hero.headline: Your buyers are searching. They can't find you yet.
hero.subtitle: 14.8 million searches happen in your category every month. Right now, XoomAI appears in zero of them — not in Google, not in ChatGPT, not in Gemini. This strategy shows exactly how to change that.
hero.oneLiner: 14.8M monthly searches. Zero results for XoomAI.

sections.heading: The Problem
sections.subtitle: XoomAI has real category expertise, but right now, buyers researching AI workflow solutions cannot find you anywhere — not in Google, not in ChatGPT, not in Gemini, not in any AI answer engine.

narrativeProse.heading: You are invisible in a market that is actively buying
narrativeProse.body: Today, XoomAI has zero organic traffic, zero ranked keywords, and zero referring domains. That is not a ranking problem — it is an existence problem. When Australian businesses search for AI workflow automation, managed AI employees, or the best AI tools for their operations, XoomAI does not appear. Not in Google results. Not in AI answer summaries. Not in any sampled prompt across ChatGPT, Gemini, or Google AI Overviews.

narrativeProse.heading: The deals you are losing happen before you know they exist
narrativeProse.body: Buyers now research, compare, and shortlist vendors across search and AI answer engines before they ever reach out. If XoomAI is not part of that conversation, you are not being rejected — you are being skipped entirely. Every day without commercial visibility is a day where prospects choose other options simply because those options were easier to find.

narrativeProse.heading: Competitors with less expertise are getting found first
narrativeProse.body: Even modest incumbents like Appenate AI Consulting (488 keywords, 396 referring domains) and Inventium AI Consulting (116 keywords, 499 referring domains) look more credible to search engines and AI models than a company with no indexed surface at all. The gap is not that competitors are universally strong — it is that they already have something buyers can find, and XoomAI does not.

stackCards.label: AI answer engines
stackCards.title: XoomAI is absent from every sampled AI recommendation
bullets: ChatGPT: 0/16 mention rate — responses default to GPT-based agents, Zapier, and UiPath.
Gemini: 0/16 mention rate — responses highlight Lindy AI and Devin-style products instead.
Google AI Overview: 0/16 mention rate — XoomAI is not cited in any detected AI Overview.

promptObservations.prompt: best managed ai employee and workflow automation
promptObservations.observed: The response leaned toward workflow tools such as Zapier and UiPath. The market is being interpreted as software selection, not as a managed service category that XoomAI could own.

promptObservations.prompt: best managed ai employee and workflow automation
promptObservations.observed: Gemini highlighted AI employee platforms such as Lindy AI and Devin-style products. XoomAI was absent, and the category was framed around product defaults rather than a managed consultancy option.

highlightBoxes.title: Caveat
highlightBoxes.body: Some additional platform probes in the research payload returned errors or timeouts. This page only uses validated ChatGPT, Gemini, and Google AI Overview observations.

sections.heading: The Opportunity
sections.eyebrow: Why XoomAI can win — and what it is worth
sections.subtitle: The market is massive, mostly unbranded, and nobody owns the managed AI consultancy niche in Australia. XoomAI does not need to outspend incumbents — it needs to show up first in one specific commercial slice.

narrativeProse.heading: The opening move is narrow, commercial, and winnable
narrativeProse.body: XoomAI should not try to win the entire AI category from day one. The highest-leverage move is the buyer-guide cluster — 140,740 validated demand around terms like 'ai tools', 'best ai', 'best ai for coding', and 'ai workflow automation tools'. These are the queries where buyers are actively choosing. Owning this layer first creates a commercial base that funds the expansion into broader category demand.

narrativeProse.heading: 97.6% of the opportunity is open market
narrativeProse.body: Non-competitor demand represents 14,494,820 of the 14,845,730 total validated opportunity. XoomAI is not forced to intercept rival brand traffic to grow. The bigger prize is becoming the default answer when buyers ask open-market research questions about AI implementation, workflow tools, and managed AI services.

narrativeProse.heading: A local frame makes the first fight easier
narrativeProse.body: Starting with managed AI employee and workflow automation research for Australian businesses gives XoomAI a clearer commercial frame, sharper page strategy, and more believable proof path than trying to dominate the word 'AI' globally from day one.

highlightBoxes.title: Estimate-only
highlightBoxes.body: The traffic curve is cumulative and aligned to the approved brief's visible 12-month base case. Revenue planning requires client ACV/AOV and funnel inputs.

sections.heading: The Plan
sections.subtitle: Three steps. Own the decision stage. Reinforce it everywhere buyers look. Then expand into the broader market.

sectionLead.takeaway: Step 1: Build the pages that answer buying questions. Step 2: Make the market hear the same story off-site. Step 3: Compound and defend.

monthBlocks.label: Month 1
monthBlocks.title: Define the opening move and ship the first decision pages
bullets: Prioritize the first commercial query set around AI tools, best AI, best AI for coding, AI workflow automation tools, and AI workflow tools.
Publish the first buyer-guide, use-case, and product-form pages aimed at Australian business buyers.
Set up schema, sitemap hygiene, canonicals, Bing Webmaster Tools, IndexNow, and baseline entity consistency.
Define the first comparison pressure against Appenate AI Consulting, Inventium AI Consulting, and Custom D.

monthBlocks.label: Month 2
monthBlocks.title: Expand comparisons, reviews, and off-site proof
bullets: Add head-to-head and evaluation pages around the highest-intent terms from Month 1.
Strengthen review-platform presence and profile consistency.
Push community participation, comparison listicles, expert commentary, and the first editorial placements.
Begin direct backlink acquisition into the early commercial winners.

monthBlocks.label: Month 3
monthBlocks.title: Deepen supporting coverage and tighten the commercial narrative
bullets: Publish supporting pages around adjacent use cases, buyer context, pricing support, and proof objects.
Improve internal linking so supporting pages feed authority into the main decision pages.
Re-test sampled prompts and search surfaces to see which claims need stronger proof.
Reinforce author, about, profile, and entity consistency across owned and third-party surfaces.

monthBlocks.label: Months 4–6
monthBlocks.title: Scale the winners and widen the market footprint
bullets: Build as many bottom-of-funnel and comparison pages as needed to cover the demand that proves worth owning.
Refresh the pages that start to win instead of only adding net-new volume.
Expand off-site authority through reviews, communities, listicles, editorials, newsletters, and more backlinks.
Move from the opening Australian-business frame into broader category demand only after the first cluster has visible traction.

scopeBlocks.label: On-site
scopeBlocks.title: Decision pages, comparisons, and supporting coverage
bullets: As many buyer-guide, best-for, use-case, and commercial decision pages as needed to cover the relevant demand.
Head-to-head comparisons, pricing-support pages, and evaluation content.
Supporting content network around adjacent use cases, buyer context, and proof objects.

scopeBlocks.label: Off-site
scopeBlocks.title: Reviews, communities, editorial, and backlinks
bullets: Review-platform reinforcement so third-party proof supports the owned pages.
Reddit and community participation where AI workflow decisions are being discussed.
Listicle pushes, digital PR, editorial placements, and aggressive backlink acquisition.
Professional-network and newsletter distribution to spread the same core message beyond search.

scopeBlocks.label: Infrastructure
scopeBlocks.title: Technical foundation and ongoing defense
bullets: Schema matched to visible content, sitemap hygiene, crawl/index eligibility, and canonicals.
Bing Webmaster Tools, IndexNow, entity consistency across owned and third-party surfaces.
Monthly measurement, prompt re-testing, refresh of winning pages, and competitive response.

points.title: Ship the first decision pages
points.detail: Month 1 establishes the first commercial territory: the buyer-guide cluster around AI tools, best AI, best AI for coding, and workflow automation terms.
bullets: Prioritize the first commercial prompt set.

points.title: Expand comparisons and trust surfaces

bullets: Launch head-to-head and evaluation pages.
Strengthen review-platform presence.
Start community, listicle, and editorial outreach.

points.title: Deepen supporting coverage
points.detail: Month 3 broadens the content network so answer engines find the same story in more places.
bullets: Ship supporting use-case and pricing-support pages.
Improve internal linking to core pages.
Re-test answer surfaces and tighten underperformers.

points.title: Reinforce first winners
points.detail: Month 4 shifts from launch to reinforcement.
bullets: Refresh early winners with stronger comparisons.
Widen community and professional distribution.
Push more backlinks into pages gaining traction.

points.title: Add proof-heavy assets
points.detail: Month 5 broadens the commercial surface with pricing and benefit-proof content.
bullets: Publish pricing-support and selection-guidance content.
Add case-evidence style proof.
Continue editorial and backlink reinforcement.

points.title: Establish first market footprint
points.detail: By Month 6, XoomAI should no longer look invisible.
bullets: Reach the first 6-month target.
Refresh winning pages rather than just adding new volume.
Use reporting to decide which themes deserve heavier expansion.

points.title: Scale into adjacent angles
points.detail: After the first footprint is established, widen into adjacent use cases and provider comparisons.
bullets: Expand decision pages around winning subtopics.
Keep review velocity active.
Patch pages that still look generic.

points.title: Increase density around use cases
points.detail: Month 8 builds retrieval density and buyer-fit clarity.
bullets: Add more use-case and persona-adjacent support pages.
Reinforce internal pathways.
Keep authority-building pointed at best themes.

points.title: Begin broader category expansion
points.detail: Push harder into broader category and brand demand.
bullets: Expand from buyer-guide wins into broader AI category capture.
Maintain commercial pages as authority center.
Keep third-party proof synchronized.

points.title: Defend owned territory
points.detail: Focus on defense as visibility grows.
bullets: Refresh where new competitors appear.
Strengthen editorial proof.
Keep structured data and entity consistency clean.

points.title: Consolidate authority
points.detail: The program should feel like a moat, not a campaign.
bullets: Continue backlink pressure into strongest pages.
Keep community and editorial coverage active.
Close final soft spots before year-end.

points.title: Finish year one with a defended position
points.detail: Month 12: visible, defensible market presence across the first important buying questions.
bullets: Reach 310,568 expected visits.
Protect best-performing decision pages.
Plan next year around adjacent categories and new proof assets.

failureBlock.heading: The cost of staying invisible
bullets: Every month without commercial pages is a month where buyers choose competitors they can actually find.
AI answer engines are training on the market right now — companies that are absent today become harder to surface tomorrow.
Even modest competitors with a few hundred referring domains will keep getting recommended over a company with no indexed presence.
The longer XoomAI waits, the more the 'managed AI employee' niche gets defined by other brands.

successBlock.eyebrow: What 12 months from now looks like
successBlock.heading: XoomAI becomes the default answer
bullets: When Australian businesses search for AI workflow automation, XoomAI shows up in Google results, ChatGPT recommendations, and Gemini answers.
The company owns the decision-stage conversation — buyer guides, comparisons, and evaluation pages that shape vendor choice.
Third-party proof (reviews, community mentions, editorial coverage) reinforces the same commercial story across every surface.
310,000+ monthly visits flowing through a defensible organic channel that compounds instead of renting attention.

cta.headline: If you want XoomAI to become the answer buyers see first
cta.body: Memetik will help turn this from a strategy memo into a live category-capture program across search, answer engines, and third-party trust surfaces.
